title: buildah panics on Fedora rawhide
body:
Reported by the multiarch team on [gitlab](https://gitlab.com/cki-project/kernel-tests/-/issues/622#note_826190324) (which seems to be down atm):
```
    podman version 4.0.0-rc1
    Podman debug info:
    host:
      arch: amd64
      buildahVersion: 1.23.1

....

         # # podman create --name wfhqPRTC606E18GnT9y0ImdJaDstzd quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 /bin/true
         # 7b032b412591530aed2a3aa8e60fef374d2db7957151bd9e38c18010e21d27a2
         # panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference

This is on a completely broken system, one on which almost every test fails with:
```
        # Error: plugin type="bridge" failed (add): cni plugin bridge failed: running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -C CNI-a5767c234fdd0783be1e797e -d 10.88.0.61/16 -j ACCEPT -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "2e5b85f0b88f8f1c49ec907899401d38f809e0e5fcd7a84b80b4449d6a244955" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.8.7 (nf_tables): Couldn't load match `comment':No such file or directory
```
(this is a known problem, and a constant source of test failures in their setup, but not one which I feel we need to look into. I merely add this in case it is relevant context).

comments.body: The stack trace looks like it is from buildah and not podman.

You're right: the actual failure is happening in an invocation of `buildah`, not  `podman`. I will try to figure out how to refile this bug into buildah.

Here's another panic, also with `buildah` in the stack trace, this time in [rc3 gating tests](http://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/24d2a23a-fb0c-487b-ba7c-c43e44ae45e6/work-tests.yml03Ql4H/tests-c7VnI8/test.podman-remote-root.bats.log):
```
# # podman-remote images --all --format {{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}} {{.ID}}
# quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610 9f9ec7f2fdef
# panic: runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference
# [signal SIGSEGV: segmentation violation code=0x1 addr=0x8 pc=0x563baa41957c]
# 
# goroutine 1 [running]:
# panic({0x563bab129000, 0x563bab8b8ae0})
# 	/usr/lib/golang/src/runtime/panic.go:941 +0x397 fp=0xc000139d80 sp=0xc000139cc0 pc=0x563baa43eff7
# runtime.panicmem(...)
# 	/usr/lib/golang/src/runtime/panic.go:220
# runtime.sigpanic()
# 	/usr/lib/golang/src/runtime/signal_unix.go:797 +0x336 fp=0xc000139dd0 sp=0xc000139d80 pc=0x563baa4557b6
# runtime.mapiternext(0xc0004eecf0?)
# 	/usr/lib/golang/src/runtime/map.go:870 +0x1c fp=0xc000139e40 sp=0xc000139dd0 pc=0x563baa41957c
# reflect.mapiternext(0xc000358cc0?)
# 	/usr/lib/golang/src/runtime/map.go:1378 +0x19 fp=0xc000139e58 sp=0xc000139e40 pc=0x563baa46a339
# github.com/modern-go/reflect2.(*UnsafeMapIterator).UnsafeNext(0x7effe5803108?)

Yep, confirmed:
```console
# buildah from quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610
[crash]
```
buildah-1.23.1-2.fc36.x86_64

5.17.0-0.rc0.20220112gitdaadb3bd0e8d.63.fc36.x86_64
Is this still happening with podman 4.0 RC3? Which includes a new version of Buildah?

Podman has nothing to do with it. I apologize for the initial false alarm, it was due to me not looking closely enough at the test source code. It's 100% buildah, 100% reproducible even on a system without podman installed.

Ok with buildah 1.24?
Can you give me a reproducer?
I did, see above. (1.23, not 1.24)

buildah from quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610
Is working for me on rootless and rootfull mode
$ rpm -q buildah
buildah-1.23.1-1.fc35.x86_64

Rawhide, not f35.

With `buildah-1.24.0-4.fc36.x86_64` and `containers-common-1-46.fc36.noarch` from koji, the crash is gone. But that version of buildah isn't even in `updates-testing` yet.

I'm not sure what's going on, probably need a c/storage heavy to look.  The failure is happening as @edsantiago noted from this command:

`# buildah from quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610`

And the path through buildah is pretty quick.  The from command is called and it asks c/storage to get the layers of the image:

Calling saveLayers() in c/storage/layers.go
https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/release-1.23/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/layers.go#L473

and then failing at: 
https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/release-1.23/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/layers.go#L484
which is simply doing:

`jldata, err := json.Marshal(&r.layers)`

Deep inside that code, it's going unhinged, I'm assuming due to finding a nil variable somewhere that it was not expecting.

How was/is that test image created?  Could it simply be a version skew between that image and what's in 1.23.1?
testimage is created by https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/905d31ddd358bb6c40082c391102bbf92a04fad6/test/system/build-testimage

@edsantiago and the image is built as part of the test run rather than something that's done in advance and set aside?

Also, it looks like you're using buildah to create the image, and then you tag it using podman.  I wonder if podman that is called is using a different c/storage?  Could you use `buildah tag` instead?

@TomSweeneyRedHat the `:20210610` in the image means "this was built on 2021-06-10". Even if I had a strong desire to build a new one today, it would not help with all the existing branches that use the `:20210610` image. This must be fixed in buildah.

And it looks like the mystery deepens.  This works in 1.23.2:

```
# ./bin/buildah from quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610
Trying to pull quay.io/libpod/testimage:20210610...

testimage-working-container

```
@TomSweeneyRedHat see above, @nalind has already opened #3763 to address
and as usual, @nalind figured it out!  Thanks Nalin, nice catch.
Weird: #3763 merged but didn't autoclose this one despite the `Fixes`

@nalind @rhatdan @edsantiago @jnovy Nalin just spun up release v1.23.3 with this fix.  This issue is present in RHEL 8.5.0.2.  Should we vendor buildah v1.23.3 into Podman v3.4..2-rhel then bump Podman and Buildah both in  RHEL 8.5.0.3?  How severe is this issue?

>Should we vendor buildah v1.23.3 into Podman [...]

Has anyone reproduced the problem using podman? AFAIK it only happens with buildah; I've never seen it in `podman build` even though all the podman tests use that same testimage.

I cannot comment on any of your other questions, sorry.

Check the version of `github.com/modern-go/reflect2` in that version of podman's `vendor/modules.txt` file.  If it's already using v1.0.2, or if we're building podman with a version of Go before 1.18, I don't think this problem is going to crop up there.

@nalind that branch of Podman has reflect2 v1.0.1 there, but I thnk it's using GoLang 1.16 to do the builds.  @cevich can you verify which version of Go is run against the Podman v3.4.2-rhel branch please?
Yes, we're running `golang-1.16.6-2.fc34` and `golang-1.15.14-2.fc33`.
OK, so we should be good for Podman v4.0 then given the above, but we probably ought to bump reflect2 up to v1.0.2 in the main branch of Podman so we don't run into this in the future.
